nodes.context: Italian-American Mafia family active in New York and New Jersey, involved in racketeering, gambling, loansharking, and labor corruption.

nodes.standard_name: Genovese crime family New Jersey faction
nodes.original_text_name: New Jersey faction of the Genovese crime family
aliases: Genovese crime family New Jersey faction
nodes.context: Subgroup of the Genovese family controlling organized crime in New Jersey, divided into crews.
New York Mafia family led by Joe Masseria, active during Prohibition and the Castellammarese War.
Brooklyn-based Mafia clan led by Salvatore Maranzano, rival to Masseria during the Castellammarese War.
New York Mafia family, later known as Lucchese family, involved in New Jersey territory division.
New York Mafia family, later known as Gambino family, involved in New Jersey operations.
New York Mafia family, involved in New Jersey territory division.
Mafia family involved in New Jersey territory division in 1937.
New York Mafia family, involved in New Jersey territory division.
Mafia family based in Philadelphia, involved in New Jersey operations and Atlantic City dispute.
New York Mafia family, rival to Genovese in Atlantic City and waterfront rackets.
New York Mafia family, allied with Genovese in some periods, involved in New Jersey.
Crew within the Genovese New Jersey faction, led by Louis Gatto and later his son Joseph Gatto, involved in gambling, loansharking, and murder.
Crew within the Genovese New Jersey faction, led by Silvio DeVita, operating in Essex County with ties to Sicily.
Crew within the Genovese New Jersey faction, led by Ludwig Bruschi, involved in gambling, loansharking, and drug distribution.
New York Mafia family led by Joe Masseria, precursor to the Genovese family, active during Prohibition.
One of the Five Families of New York, allied with the Genovese family.
New Jersey Mafia family, allied with the Genovese family.
Mafia family based in Detroit, allied with the Genovese family.

nodes.standard_name: Lucchese crime family New Jersey faction
nodes.context: New Jersey faction of the Lucchese crime family, allied with the Genovese family.
Philadelphia-based Irish-American gang, allied with the Genovese family.
Crew within the Genovese New Jersey faction, led by Tino Fiumara and later Stephen Depiro, controlling waterfront operations.
Brooklyn-based crew within the Genovese family, led by Jimmy Napoli, later taken over by Anthony Palumbo.

edges.context: The New Jersey faction is a subgroup of the Genovese crime family.
The two families fought the Castellammarese War for control of Italian gangs in the U.S.

edges.source: Genovese crime family New Jersey faction
edges.context: Early members of the New Jersey faction were under the control of Joe Masseria's family.
The families disputed over Atlantic City territory and Manna plotted to kill John Gotti.
Genovese gained support from Lucchese boss Tommy Lucchese in the 1950s power struggle.
Genovese planned to take over Bruno's operations after his murder, leading to conflict.
